title: eriostemma samarense
intro: Eriostemma samarense is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Traditionally, Eriostemma samarense is used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antidote for snake bites. Eriostemma samarense is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific research and validation.
